sources_exist.note: All four URL-bearing citation occurrences resolve to substantive live ASHI pages.

figures_match_sources.note: Each quoted passage appears verbatim and in context; the citation scopes accurately distinguish panel examples and vendor-software pricing from representative firm adoption and operator service pricing.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The role fractions are bounded and mechanically plausible, but the record labels them DERIVED while admitting they are judgmental and providing no reproducible bridge from anecdotal home-inspector task examples to the eleven selected fractions.

rubric_consistent.note: The v3.1 provenance contract is violated by disguised estimates in ai_replaceable_share and pricing_structure; the latter cites vendor software cost formats rather than evidence for the operator transaction-fee structure used in capture reasoning.

cross_checks_honest.note: The cross-checks candidly call the operator transaction fee a model assumption, keep PE/M&A out of V/A, separate cost reduction from product survival, and map pricing pressure into pi_dist and pi_moat.

source_audits.note: The live ASHI page states verbatim that a panelist's team uses AI to draft narratives, identify components from inspection photos, and reference building codes; adjacent text also supports scheduling, email and marketing examples. It provides task examples, not occupational task-time shares.
The live November 2022 ASHI article states that most home inspectors use report-writing software and describes the transition from handwritten and template-based reports to digital reporting.

The live ASHI page contains the exact quoted AI-assisted narrative, photo-component and building-code task examples; the record correctly limits the analogue to workflow assistance rather than prevalence or replacement.

The live ASHI article reports a survey of 79 inspectors and contains the exact quoted per-report, monthly, annual and one-time software-charge formats. This supports vendor software cost only, not the operator's inspection service price.

reasons: inputs.ai_replaceable_share.provenance_type is DERIVED, but inputs.ai_replaceable_share.derivation explicitly says the eleven within-role fractions are judgmental, and the cited ASHI webinar supplies only practitioner task examples with no occupational task-time shares or reproducible rule mapping those examples to the selected fractions. Correct the rerun by treating the citation only as background for an ESTIMATE or by supplying representative task-time evidence and a complete reproducible mapping to each role fraction.

inputs.pricing_structure.provenance_type is DERIVED even though inputs.pricing_structure.derivation admits that the score-relevant flat transaction-fee characterization is an industry-model judgment; its citation establishes only the vendor pricing of report-writing software. Correct the rerun by citing evidence for the operator service-pricing model and deriving the characterization from it, or classify the unsourced operator-pricing characterization as an estimate and keep the vendor-cost fact separate.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 8 inputs with provenance_type ESTIMATE (active_consolidators, buy_mult, current_adoption_pct, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct, pi_dist, pi_moat, t50_years)
